title: Mayor Walsh Announces Elmwood Park Sediment Removal Project
summary: Mayor Walsh announced a sediment removal and restoration project for Elmwood Park's Furnace Brook, running June–October 2025, funded by FEMA and NYS emergency grants. The project aims to restore the brook to pre-Tropical Storm Fred (2021) conditions through dredging, channel wall repair, and turf restoration. The park will be closed during construction, with moderate traffic impacts on Glenwood Avenue.
department_refs: Department of Parks, Recreation and Youth Programs

entity_refs.name: NYS Department of Homeland Security and Emergency Services

political_signal.description: Environmental restoration and disaster recovery prioritized; successful grant coordination with state and federal emergency agencies demonstrates intergovernmental collaboration.

processing_notes: Author listed as 'Unknown' in source metadata; actual author likely Office of Communications.
Project duration spans May–October window to protect trout spawning season (Carpenter's Brook Fish Hatchery partnership noted).
No dollar amounts provided in release; grants from FEMA and NYS DHSES are source.
Furnace Brook is tributary ecosystem element worth tracking for water quality/GIS integration.
Park closure duration June–October 2025 may warrant 311/Parks service communication planning.
